quantification.why: Essentially every comparison carries a computed figure, e.g. Gadget is "33.4% of revenue, from only 16 units" and "West revenue equals 65.0% of North's, the widest regional gap."

specificity.why: Each recommendation names a product/region with its figures, e.g. "Review Sprocket pricing; 238 units generate only $1,130.50" and "Investigate the West region; at $1,414.82 it trails North ($2,176.35) by 35.0%."

insight_density.why: It surfaces multiple non-obvious, actionable points—revenue concentration/per-unit efficiency ("$2,392.00... from only 16 units"), the widest regional gap (65.0%), and that Sprocket's "high volume does not offset the low $4.75 price."

quantification.why: Essentially every comparison carries a computed figure, e.g. "West revenue equals 65.0% of North's" and "trails North ($2,176.35) by 35.0%".

specificity.why: Every recommendation names a product/region with figures, e.g. "Review Sprocket pricing; 238 units generate only $1,130.50" and "Investigate the West region; at $1,414.82 it trails North."

insight_density.why: Multiple actionable observations on concentration, gaps, and outliers, e.g. Gadget's "33.4% of revenue from only 16 units" and Sprocket's "high volume does not offset the low $4.75 price."

quantification.why: Essentially every comparison carries a computed figure — e.g. 'Gadget leads with $2,392.00, or 33.4% of revenue' and 'West revenue equals 65.0% of North's, the widest regional gap.'

specificity.why: Every recommendation names a product/region with data — e.g. 'Investigate the West region; at $1,414.82 it trails North ($2,176.35) by 35.0%' and 'Review Sprocket pricing; 238 units generate only $1,130.50.'

insight_density.why: Surfaces multiple actionable, non-obvious observations: revenue concentration (Gadget's 33.4% from 16 units at highest revenue-per-unit), the widest regional gap (West at 65.0% of North), and the volume-vs-revenue outlier (Sprocket's 238 units yielding the least).
